summary: The build failed because a `wget` command inside the Dockerfile could not download a Nydus release tarball from GitHub, exiting with code 1. This caused buildkit to abort the solve operation. The wget failure appears to be caused by a context cancellation that also interrupted in-flight layer fetches from quay.io.
root_cause: The RUN step executing `wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz` exited with code 1, causing the buildkit solve to fail. Concurrent layer fetches from quay.io were also canceled via context cancellation, indicating the build context was terminated (likely due to a network or timeout condition) before all operations completed.

evidence.quote: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the direct error reported by buildkit: the wget RUN instruction failed with a non-zero exit code, which is the proximate cause of the build failure.

evidence.quote: level=error msg="/moby.buildkit.v1.Control/Solve returned error: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1"
evidence.reason: The buildkit gRPC Solve RPC returned an error wrapping the wget failure, confirming this is what terminated the build.

evidence.quote: level=debug msg="fetch failed" digest="sha256:0288b5a0d7e71a2cdbdf114cb9869ce2bc4b05adfd6aa5e3ee9fa4b3847e7b80" error="failed to do request: Get \"https://cdn01.quay.io/...\"`: context canceled"
evidence.reason: Layer fetches were being canceled via context cancellation around the same time, consistent with the build context being aborted after the wget step failed.

evidence.quote: level=error msg="/moby.buildkit.v1.Control/Solve returned error: rpc error: code = Canceled desc = context canceled"
evidence.reason: A second Solve RPC was also canceled, indicating cascading cancellation across concurrent build operations after the primary failure.

suggested_fix: Investigate why `wget` failed to download the Nydus release from `https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz` — check whether the specified `$NYDUS_VERSION` tag exists for the target `$TARGETOS`/`$TARGETARCH` combination, and whether GitHub's release download endpoint was reachable from the CI runner at the time of the build. If this is a transient network failure, re-running the job may resolve it.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.

summary: The test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub during the test run. GitHub returned repeated 502 errors, exhausting all retries. This is a transient network failure, not a code defect.
root_cause: GitHub's servers returned repeated 502 responses when pip attempted to download `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`, causing `Max retries exceeded` and a non-zero exit code that failed the test assertion.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Explicitly shows the network failure: GitHub returned 502 responses until retries were exhausted, which is a transient server-side error.
The test was retried three times and failed each time, confirming a persistent transient network condition rather than a deterministic code bug.
The sole failing test is the one that contacts GitHub, and its failure is an AssertionError triggered by the non-zero return code caused by the network error.
Confirms the test exercise requires a live outbound HTTPS connection to github.com, making it susceptible to external network failures.

suggested_fix: Re-run the failed job; the 502 errors from GitHub are transient. Consider adding retry logic or marking this test with a network-flaky marker so it is automatically re-run on network failures without failing the entire CI run.

summary: A single Jest test assertion failed in test/config/get.ts because pnpm's `config get` command returned a registry URL with a trailing slash ('https://my-org.registry.example.com/') while the test expected no trailing slash ('https://my-org.registry.example.com'). All other 475 tests passed.
root_cause: The `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` command outputs the registry URL with a trailing slash appended, but the test asserts strict equality against the URL without a trailing slash.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Shows the value the test expected — a URL without a trailing slash
Shows the actual value returned by the command — a URL with a trailing slash, causing the strict equality check to fail
Identifies the exact assertion at test/config/get.ts:32 that failed
Confirms exactly one test failed across the entire suite, isolating the root cause to this single assertion

suggested_fix: Either update the test at test/config/get.ts:32 to accept a trailing slash (change the expected value to 'https://my-org.registry.example.com/'), or fix the `config get` output in the pnpm source to strip trailing slashes from registry URLs before printing them.
